transcript:
Agent: Thank you for calling BrightTech Support. My name is Sarah, how can I help you today?

Customer: Hi Sarah. I'm really frustrated. I just got my bill and it shows a charge of $150 for a service I never signed up for. This is completely unacceptable!

Agent: I completely understand your frustration, Mr. Torres, and I sincerely apologize for this inconvenience. Let me pull up your account right away. Can I have your account number or the email on file?

Customer: It's michael.torres@email.com.

Agent: Thank you. I can see your account here. You are right — there is an additional charge for our Premium Cloud Storage plan added on April 15th. It looks like this was added during a promotional campaign. Did you receive any emails about this offer?

Customer: Absolutely not. I never agreed to this. I want it removed immediately and a refund.

Agent: Of course, Mr. Torres. I am removing that charge right now and processing a full refund of $150 to your credit card ending in 4521. The refund should appear within 3 to 5 business days.

Customer: Okay, good. But I want to make sure this never happens again.

Agent: Absolutely. I have added a note to your account that no promotional services should be added without your explicit written consent. I am also escalating this to our billing team so they can investigate how this was applied. You will receive a confirmation email shortly. Is there anything else I can help you with?

Customer: No, that covers it. Thank you, Sarah. I appreciate how quickly you handled this.

Agent: You are very welcome! I am sorry again for the inconvenience, Mr. Torres. Have a great rest of your day!
